name: Chronic Obstructive Pulmonary Disease (COPD)
summary: COPD is characterized by persistent airflow limitation and chronic lung inflammation. GENARCH provides educational population-level summaries only.
adolescent_relevance: Typically diagnosed later in life; included to illustrate exposure-driven lung remodeling mechanisms at a population level.

genetic_architecture.prs_notes: PRS discussed as population-level genetic liability; GENARCH does not provide individual predictions.

exposure_modifiers.mechanism_hypothesis: Smoking is a dominant population-level driver of COPD burden.
Ambient particulates can contribute to chronic airway irritation in cohorts.
Occupational particulates contribute in some settings, often interacting with smoking.

population_equity.gwas_ancestry_breakdown: Many COPD GWAS are European-ancestry dominated historically.
population_equity.transferability_notes: Exposure distributions vary across populations, affecting interpretation.
population_equity.data_gaps: Phenotype definitions and exposure measurement vary across cohorts.

references.title: Alpha-1 antitrypsin deficiency and COPD: genetics and mechanisms (review)
references.journal: American Journal of Respiratory and Critical Care Medicine

references.title: The Health Consequences of Smoking—50 Years of Progress
references.journal: U.S. Department of Health and Human Services

references.title: Air pollution and health: evidence and mechanisms (review)
references.journal: Journal of the Air & Waste Management Association

references.title: Occupational exposures and chronic lung disease risk: overview (review)
